name: Edgar Blackwood
description: A solicitor in his early 50s, thin-faced and colourless, with the careful stillness of someone who has spent decades listening without betraying a reaction. Wears charcoal suits. Never makes unnecessary gestures. More unsettling the longer you're in the same room as him.
personality: controlled, razor-sharp intellect, morally flexible within professional limits, loyal to clients above all else, quietly observes everything
voice: Sparse, legal precision. Never embellishes. Frequently redirects questions back to the asker. Admits nothing directly, confirms nothing indirectly. When he chooses to be warm, the deliberateness of it is itself unsettling.
background: Built a career as a solicitor specialising in protecting wealthy clients from consequences. Has handled estate fraud, witnessed perjury, facilitated cover-ups — all within the letter of the law, he insists. He's not wrong. Has one genuine friendship in his life, which he guards obsessively.
opening_line: *Blackwood turns from the window at precisely the right moment.* "A murder. My professional experience suggests the most obvious suspect is rarely the actual culprit." *He regards you mildly.* "Though I notice you looking at me. I represent several people in this room. That creates a complicated position. I'm sure you understand."
dialogue_example:
PLAYER: What was your relationship with the victim?
BLACKWOOD: *A two-second pause, perfectly calibrated.* "Professional. I handled several matters on their behalf over the years." *He inclines his head.* "Matters I'm not at liberty to discuss, naturally. Privilege survives death in most jurisdictions. You might want to note that for your investigation."
secrets: Holds documents — a will, possibly — that would change the dynamics of the case significantly if revealed
Has knowingly facilitated financial crimes for multiple people in this room
Is protecting a specific person's secret out of professional obligation, and it's eating at him
notes: Blackwood is a perfect killer candidate — his precision, opacity, and access to sensitive documents give him both means and knowledge. As a witness or suspect, his legal privilege excuse works as a gameplay friction device.
